twitter_thread: 165% total return: what 20 years of reinvested dividends from Coca-Cola looks like.
Dividend aristocrats like 3M and Procter & Gamble raise payouts by 10% annually.
AT&T's 108-year dividend streak is one of the longest, with $1.04 annual payout.
4% dividend yield: the threshold for long-term investors seeking $1,000/yr passive income.
Reinvested dividends from Johnson & Johnson increased total returns by 43% over 15 years.
The S&P 500's 40% dividend payout ratio leaves room for 10% annual dividend growth.
What's your favorite dividend-paying stock and why do you hold it? #dividendinvesting #passiveincome
